data.title: Amazon Stock (AMZN) Slides on Gaming Head's Departure
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN) stock was down on Thursday alongside news that the head of its gaming division is leaving the company. According to these reports, Amazon Game Studios leader Christoph Hartmann will leave the company as it switches focus away from developing games for PC and consoles. Instead, Amazon will reportedly focus more on its Luna game streaming platform.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Hartmann's reported departure comes at the same time that Amazon has revealed massive job cuts. The company said it will cut 16,000 jobs, though it didn't clarify if its gaming division was included. Amazon Game Studio already faced layoffs late last year, and the company has announced the end of life for its New World MMORPG.

All of this shows that Amazon wasn't cut out for the video game market. Its internal projects couldn't attract enough players to warrant continuation, and the company failed to establish itself as a competent PC game storefront, despite its strong focus on e-commerce. Amazon attempted to be the next Steam, the leading PC game storefront, but failed to event make a dent in the market.

Amazon Stock Movement Today

Amazon stock was down 2.19% on Thursday but was still up 2.99% year-to-date. The company's shares have also rallied 3.57% over the past 12 months.

Amazon stock movement today saw some 17 million shares change hands, compared to a three-month daily average trading volume of about 34.77 million units.
data.author: William White

data.title: Carvana Stock (CVNA) Rallies after JPMorgan Defends It from Short Seller
data.description:
Shares of online used-car retailer Carvana (CVNA) are rallying today after Wall Street analysts came to the company's defense following a short report from Gotham City Research. Notably, that report had knocked the stock down by about 14% the day before, as it raised questions about Carvana's financial practices. However, five-star JPMorgan (JPM) analyst Rajat Gupta, who has a Buy rating and a $510 price target on Carvana, said that the short report misunderstood key financial numbers.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

According to Gupta, the report mixed long-term, cumulative figures with annual results, which led to incorrect conclusions. In addition, he said he was surprised by how sharp the stock's drop was, especially given what he described as fairly straightforward math related to asset-backed securities and well-known challenges from 2022 and 2023 loans. He also said the report wrongly described how service income from DriveTime flows through Carvana's loan-related businesses.

Unsurprisingly, Carvana strongly rejected the accusations as well. In a statement on Wednesday, a company spokesperson said that the report was inaccurate and intentionally misleading. The company stated that all related-party transactions are fully disclosed in its financial statements. Carvana also reminded investors that it plans to release its 2025 earnings on February 18, 2026, which suggests that upcoming results should help address investor concerns.

Is CVNA Stock a Good Buy?

Overall, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on CVNA stock based on 16 Buys, two Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average CVNA price target of $790.88 per share implies 87.6% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: NVIDIA Links Quantum Computing's Future to Its GPU Platform
data.description:
NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) published a policy-focused post on Wednesday that offers a clear signal about how the next phase of quantum development may unfold in the U.S. Rather than spotlighting narrow lab milestones, the company framed quantum computing as part of a broader system that already includes AI and high-performance computing. As a result, the message carries more weight for investors tracking long-term platform strategy than for readers focused on near-term scientific progress.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For years, quantum computing has been difficult to place in a market context. Hardware progress remains early, timelines are uncertain, and few companies show a clear path to commercial scale. However, NVIDIA's post presents a different view, where quantum value comes from how it connects to existing computing systems rather than from standalone breakthroughs.

At the center of the post is a call for Congress to reauthorize the National Quantum Initiative, a program launched in 2018 to coordinate U.S. quantum research across national labs, universities, and industry. According to NVIDIA, the original strategy now needs an update because AI has become essential to making quantum systems useful at scale. The company argues that quantum and AI should no longer be treated as separate efforts but instead combined into what it describes as a new class of scientific machines.

As NVIDIA wrote, "AI and quantum computing are no longer just distinct tools, but the foundational elements of a new class of supercomputers." These systems would blend GPUs, CPUs, and quantum processors into a single environment designed to solve complex scientific problems.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: SOUN Stock Drops despite Five Guys Partnership Expansion
data.description:
SoundHound AI (SOUN) stock dipped despite news that the company expanded its partnership with Five Guys. The deal boosts SOUN's presence in enterprise AI, but investors remain cautious, keeping the stock under pressure. Following the announcement, SOUN fell 2% yesterday and is down another 4.5% so far today.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For context, SoundHound AI specializes in voice recognition and natural language processing, offering AI-driven solutions across industries. Meanwhile, Five Guys is a fast-casual burger chain known with hundreds of locations across the U.S. and internationally.

More Details on the Partnership

The renewal shows the strength of the partnership, as SoundHound's AI-powered ordering agents have already handled over a million customer interactions for Five Guys. Under the renewed deal, Five Guys will let franchisees expand use of the AI technology.

Using SoundHound's voice AI, the chain can take 100% of orders, even during busy times, reducing missed orders and letting customers order quickly and accurately. For staff, this means fewer interruptions, allowing them to focus on in-store service, food prep, and delivering a great guest experience.

What This Means for SoundHound

The expanded partnership with Five Guys strengthens SoundHound's position in the enterprise AI market and highlights the growing demand for its voice AI technology. The system has already handled customer interactions, enabling faster and more accurate ordering and demonstrating its value in real-world settings.

The deal also paves the way for wider adoption across franchise locations, potentially boosting revenue and visibility for SoundHound—even as investors watch the stock closely for short-term swings.

Is SOUN a Good Stock to Buy?

According to TipRanks, SOUN stock has received a Moderate Buy consensus rating, with five Buys and two Holds assigned in the last three months. The average SoundHound stock price target is $16.60, suggesting a potential upside of 83.53% from the current level.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Polymarket Odds: Will AXP, XOM, VZ Beat Earnings Tomorrow?
data.description:
On Friday, American Express (AXP), Exxon Mobil (XOM), and Verizon (VZ) will wrap up an eventful week of earnings. AXP and XOM will report before the market opens, while Verizon's earnings will come after the closing bell. Traders are already weighing in on their expectations through Polymarket.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

  • American Express is expected to report an EPS of $3.53
  • Exxon Mobil is expected to report an adjusted EPS of $1.69
  • Verizon is expected to report an adjusted EPS of $1.05

What is Polymarket?

Polymarket is a prediction platform where traders bet on the outcomes of real-world events, including earnings reports, elections, and economic indicators. Polymarket odds reflect expectations and can provide insight into investor sentiment.

Will American Express Beat Earnings?

American Express has beaten earnings during 15 of the last 18 quarters. Polymarket traders are confident that the financial services giant will top its estimated EPS of $3.53, giving it an 88% chance.

Will Exxon Mobil Beat Earnings?

Exxon Mobil has beaten earnings 77.77% of the time during the past 18 quarters. That nearly lines up with the 79% odds of the oil and gas leader surpassing its estimated adjusted EPS of $1.69 this quarter on Polymarket.

Will Verizon Beat Earnings?

Verizon has beaten earnings during 16 of the past 18 quarters for an 88.88% success rate. Polymarket gives the company an 80% chance of surpassing its estimated adjusted EPS of $1.05.

It's worth pointing out that Polymarket odds reflect expectations, not guaranteed outcomes. The odds represent the views and expectations of traders, but actual earnings results can differ significantly from these predictions. Investors should treat Polymarket data as just one tool when evaluating their investment decisions.

data.title: AMD & Qualcomm (QCOM) Stocks Slip Despite Reported SOCAMM2 AI Plans
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock was down on Thursday despite reports that the company would adopt SOCAMM2 memory for its line of artificial intelligence (AI)-focused products. According to the report from TechPowerUp, SOCAMM2 would enhance the performance of AI components, allowing the chipmaker to better compete against other rivals in the space, such as Nvidia (NVDA).

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The rumor also notes that it isn't just AMD that may be considering adding SOCAMM2 memory to its AI product line. Semiconductor company Qualcomm (QCOM) is also among the reported companies that have weighed adding the technology to its AI offerings. It's also worth noting that Nvidia has already implemented this technology in its AI offerings with its Vera CPU.

Considering Nvidia's domination of the AI hardware space, it makes sense that AMD and Qualcomm would follow its move to add SOCAMM2 memory to their AI products.

AMD & QCOM Stock Movements Today

AMD stock was down 2.03% on Thursday but was still up 15.57% year-to-date. The stock has also rallied 112.64% over the past 12 months. Some 7.7 million shares of AMD have changed hands today, compared to a three-month daily average trading volume of about 30 million units.

Qualcomm stock was down 0.96% on Thursday and has fallen 11.61% year-to-date. The stock has also dropped 11.18% over the past 12 months. Roughly 1.3 million shares of QCOM stock have been traded today, compared to a three-month daily average trading volume of around 7.6 million shares.
data.author: William White

data.title: XRP Price Sees Big Catalysts as Millionaire Wallets End 4 Months of Selling & ETFs Boom
data.description:
XRP (XRP-USD) has faced a rocky start to the year, with its price falling about 4% throughout January. However, behind the scenes, a different story is playing out. Large-scale investors, often called whales, are using this period of weakness to rebuild their positions. On-chain data indicates that the number of wallets holding at least one million XRP has begun to climb again, marking a significant shift in behavior after months of steady selling.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Millionaire Wallets End 4 Months of Selling

The total count of these millionaire addresses rose by 42 this month, bringing the total to 2,016 globally. This is the first time this metric has increased since September 2025. According to the analytics firm Santiment, the return of these high-value holders to the ledger is an "encouraging sign for the long-term." At current prices, each of these wallets holds at least $1.87 million worth of the digital asset, showing a massive level of financial commitment from these participants.

Investors Favor XRP ETFs Over Bitcoin

While the broader crypto market has been struggling, XRP investment products are seeing a massive surge in demand. U.S.-listed spot XRP ETFs have pulled in a net $91.72 million so far this month. This trend stands in sharp contrast to Bitcoin ETFs, which have actually seen investors pull out $278 million over the same period.

The steady flow of money into these regulated funds suggests that institutional players are treating XRP as a portfolio staple rather than a speculative trade. Total assets held across all XRP spot funds now stand at roughly $1.51 billion. This institutional buying has helped create a "floor" for the price, even as individual traders remain cautious.

Smart Money Absorbs Market Pressure

Data from Nansen reveals that "smart money" traders, those with a history of high returns, have increased their XRP holdings by more than 11% over the last 30 days. This quiet accumulation suggests that professional investors are looking past the current price of $1.81 and focusing on the future.

Historically, when the number of large holders rises while the price stays flat or falls, it signals a period of accumulation. Essentially, the weak hands are selling their tokens while strong hands are scooping them up. Many analysts believe that once this period of consolidation ends, XRP could be primed for a sharp move toward the $2.30 level, provided the broader market finds its footing.

At the time of writing, XRP is sitting at $1.8107.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Why Is MP Materials Stock (MP) Falling Today?
data.description:
U.S.-based MP Materials (MP) is down by roughly 10% as of this writing, extending a volatile week for the stock. The latest dip came after Reuters reported that the Trump administration has stepped back from plans to guarantee a minimum price for U.S. critical minerals projects, citing limited congressional funding and the challenge of setting market prices. For MP Materials, the policy shift removes a potential layer of downside protection the market had been expecting.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

MP Materials pushed back on the market reports, calling them "inaccurate, misleading, and inconsistent with the facts." The company said it continues to have a binding, long-term agreement with the U.S. Department of War that remains fully in place, including the price protection agreement signed last year. MP added that there has been no change to its contract or to the government's obligations under it.

What's Happening with MP Materials Stock?

Shares of MP Materials swung sharply this week. The moves were triggered by news that the U.S. government had awarded a major funding package to rival USA Rare Earth (USAR), raising questions about whether increased support for a competitor could hurt MP's long-term outlook.

The stock sold off hard on Monday, dropping more than 10%. By Tuesday, sentiment had shifted. MP Materials climbed back, as investors were growing more confident that rising demand for rare earth elements—driven by defense spending and the energy transition—can support more than one major supplier in North America.

What the Policy Shift Could Mean for MP Materials

The rumored policy change takes away a safety net investors were counting on. A guaranteed minimum price would have helped protect revenue when rare earth prices fall, so backing away from that plan adds more uncertainty around future price support.

That said, the move does not affect MP's existing contracts or current operations. MP remains the leading U.S. producer of rare earth materials, and long-term demand tied to defense, EVs, and clean energy still supports the broader investment case.

In the near term, however, the lack of clear government price guarantees could keep the stock volatile.

Is MP Materials a Good Stock to Buy?

According to TipRanks, MP stock has received a Strong Buy consensus rating, with 11 Buys assigned in the last three months. The average MP Materials' stock price target is $76.13, suggesting an upside of 13.6% from the current level.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: This Is Why Serina Therapeutics Stock (SER) Rocketed Today
data.description:
Serina Therapeutics (SER) stock rocketed on Thursday after the pharmaceutical company announced clearance from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) for its Investigational New Drug (IND) application for SER-252. This is an investigational therapy for advanced Parkinson's disease.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Serina Therapeutics noted that this approval allows the company to move forward with a Phase 1b registrational clinical study evaluating SER-252 in patients with advanced Parkinson's disease. This follows several communications with the FDA for the trial in an effort to reach a New Drug Application (NDA) for SER-252.

Steve Ledger, CEO of Serina Therapeutics, said, "FDA clearance of the IND is a major milestone for Serina and underscores the promise of the SER-252 program. As we initiate our registrational study, we will begin generating meaningful clinical data for SER-252 in patients with advanced Parkinson's disease. With FDA alignment on a registrational development strategy under a 505(b)(2) NDA pathway, we believe SER-252 has a clear and efficient path forward toward addressing a significant unmet medical need."

Serina Therapeutics Stock Movement Today

Serina Therapeutics stock was up 37.87% on Thursday, extending a 96.03% rally year-to-date. However, the shares were still down 40.87% over the past 12 months.

With today's news came heavy trading of SER stock, as more than 45 million shares changed hands, compared to a three-month daily average trading volume of about 24,000 units.
data.author: William White
